title: ZimerfeldTree - Restore
treeHint: Brings the ENTIRE tracked content of the chosen commit in as staged changes; history is untouched.
revertHint: Creates a NEW commit that undoes the chosen commit, without rewriting history — safe on a shared/pushed branch.
informHashAndName: Enter the commit hash and the new branch/tag name.
confirmInspectWarn:
This will check out the commit in "detached HEAD" mode (inspection only). No branch is moved.

To return, check out a branch from the main window. Do you want to continue?
informReflogAndName: Select the reflog entry and enter the branch name.
confirmReflogResetWarn:
WARNING: git reset --hard will move the current branch '{0}' to the reflog entry '{1}' and DISCARD later commits/changes.

This action is IRREVERSIBLE. Do you want to continue?
discardHint: Discards working-tree changes (does not touch history). Use it to take the code back to the last commit.
confirmDiscardUnstaged:
This will discard the UNSTAGED changes to tracked files (git checkout -- .).

Staged changes and untracked files are preserved. Do you want to continue?
confirmDiscardHard:
WARNING: git reset --hard HEAD will discard ALL staged and unstaged changes to tracked files.

This action is IRREVERSIBLE. Do you want to continue?
confirmClean:
WARNING: git clean -fd will permanently remove ALL untracked files and folders.

Tracked and ignored files are not affected. This action is IRREVERSIBLE. Do you want to continue?
rebaseHint: ADVANCED — rewrites history. Never use on a branch that is already shared/pushed.
confirmRebaseDrop:
WARNING: this rewrites history, removing commit '{0}' and replaying the later ones (git rebase --onto).

NEVER do this on a branch others already have. On conflict, resolve and finish, or use "Abort Rebase". Do you want to continue?
rebaseConflictHint: *** The rebase may have stopped due to a CONFLICT. Resolve the conflicts and finish (git rebase --continue), or click "Abort Rebase" to return to the previous state. ***
informHashAndFile: Enter the commit hash and the file path.
informHashOrRange: Enter the commit hash or range.
confirmHardWarn:
WARNING: git reset --hard will discard ALL uncommitted files in '{0}'.

This action is IRREVERSIBLE. Do you want to continue?
confirmResetTagWarn:
WARNING: git reset --hard will move the branch '{0}' to the tag '{1}' and DISCARD all later commits and changes.

This action is IRREVERSIBLE. Do you want to continue?
aboutBody:
RESTORE — ways to go back in time in your code

Each tab is a way to recover or undo a repository state. They are ordered from the safest to the most destructive. Buttons in red are irreversible and ask for confirmation.

══════════════════════════════════════════
🟢 SAFE (do not rewrite history)
══════════════════════════════════════════

• Emergency Plan — takes a branch to the state of a tag (release).
    Restore to Tag → git checkout <tag> -- .
      Brings the tag's content in as staged changes; history untouched.
    Reset to Tag → git reset --hard <tag>  (moves the pointer; discards later work).

• Restore File — recovers ONE file from an old commit.
    git checkout <hash> -- <file>   (changes are left staged)
    The "Browse…" button opens the Windows file picker already in the project folder; a file outside the repository root is rejected.

• Restore Tree — recovers the ENTIRE tracked content of a commit.
    git checkout <hash> -- .   (changes are left staged; history untouched)

• Cherry-Pick — applies one or more commits onto the current branch.
    git cherry-pick <hash>      (range: <old-hash>..<recent-hash>)

• Revert — creates a NEW commit that undoes an earlier commit.
    Revert Commit → git revert <hash>
    Revert Merge  → git revert -m 1 <hash>  (undoes a whole merge)
    This is the CORRECT way to undo something already pushed to the remote.

• New Branch/Tag — creates a branch or tag pointing at an old commit.
    git branch <name> <hash>   /   git tag <name> <hash>
    "Forks off" the past without touching any existing branch.

══════════════════════════════════════════
🔵 INSPECT (read-only)
══════════════════════════════════════════

• Inspect (on the New Branch/Tag tab) — git checkout <hash>
    Opens the code exactly as it was at that commit, in "detached HEAD". No branch is moved; return by checking out a branch.

══════════════════════════════════════════
🟡 RECOVERY
══════════════════════════════════════════

• Recover (Reflog) — lists every movement of HEAD (commit, reset, rebase, checkout, merge).
    Create Branch Here → git branch <name> <entry>
    Reset Current Here → git reset --hard <entry>
    Use it to recover a "lost" commit after a bad reset --hard, or to rebuild a branch deleted by mistake.

══════════════════════════════════════════
🟠 DISCARD LOCAL CHANGES (working tree)
══════════════════════════════════════════

• Discard Local — throws away UNCOMMITTED changes (does not touch history).
    Discard unstaged   → git checkout -- .
    Reset --hard HEAD  → git reset --hard HEAD   (staged + unstaged)
    Remove untracked   → git clean -fd

══════════════════════════════════════════
🔴 REWRITE HISTORY (advanced)
══════════════════════════════════════════

• Reset Branch — moves the branch pointer to an earlier commit.
    --mixed  undoes commits, keeps changes as unstaged (default)
    --soft   undoes commits, keeps changes as staged
    --hard   undoes commits and DISCARDS all local changes

• Rebase — removes a specific commit from history.
    git rebase --onto <hash>^ <hash>   (replays the later commits onto the parent)
    On conflict, resolve and run git rebase --continue, or click "Abort Rebase".

⚠️  NEVER rewrite (Reset --hard, Rebase) the history of a branch other people already have — it breaks your teammates' repository.

══════════════════════════════════════════
👥 TEAMWORK
══════════════════════════════════════════

Multiple developers on the SAME branch (e.g. main):
  • To undo something ALREADY PUSHED, use Revert (not Reset --hard): it creates an undo commit without rewriting history others already pulled.
  • Run "git pull" (preferably --rebase) BEFORE pushing, to avoid the non-fast-forward rejection.
  • Reset --hard, Rebase and Discard are only safe on LOCAL work nobody else has.

Multiple branches to be merged into develop:
  • Bring specific commits from one branch into another with Cherry-Pick (without merging everything).
  • If a merge into develop went wrong, undo it with Revert Merge (-m 1) — it preserves the rest of the history.
  • Resolve conflicts calmly; for a stuck rebase/merge, "Abort Rebase" (or git merge --abort) returns to the previous state.
  • To isolate or resume old work, create a branch from the commit (New Branch/Tag).

══════════════════════════════════════════
How to find a commit hash
══════════════════════════════════════════

  git log --oneline             lists every commit from HEAD
  git log --oneline -20         last 20 commits
  git log --oneline <branch>    commits of a specific branch
  git log --oneline A..B        commits in B that are not in A
  git log --oneline --all       commits of every branch
  git reflog                    history of HEAD movements

The hash dropdowns list recent commits as (date) [branch] hash → message, newest first. You can select an item or type any hash manually.
